english: on, keeping to) relative to, with respect to, in consequence of; also dé-la (de la) without rtén-nas (rten nas) id.; with verbs expressing feelings of the mind: at, off, concerning etc., dé-la dgá-ste (de la dga' ste) glad of, rejoicing at it; sdig-pa-la dzém-pa (sdig pa la 'dzem pa) to be afraid of sin; ma byuṅ-ba ’di-la gdams-ṅag gsól-to (ma byung ba 'di la gdams ngag gsol to) he asked advice with respect to this not having been done Mil.; in introducing a new subject: rgyál-sa me-nyág-la śór-bai lo-rgyús-la (rgyal sa me nyag la shor ba'i lo rgyus la) now, as to the fact of the supremacy having been transferred to Taṅ-yul (Tang yul), it... Glr.; in headings of chapters etc., e.g. gliṅ bźii miṅ-la (gling bzhi'i ming la) names of the four parts of the globe Trig.; čé-čuṅ-gi tsad-la (che chung gi tshad la) with respect to size Glr.; bre-sráṅ-la yyo mi byéd-pa (bre srang la g.yo mi byed pa) not to cheat by measure and weight Glr.; for the Latin erga and contra, as: dgra-la rgol-ba (dgra la rgol ba) to struggle against or with an enemy; bu-la snyiṅ-brtse-nas (bu la snying brtse nas) from love to her son; nad-stóbs-kyi čé-čuṅ-la dpág-pa (nad stobs kyi che chung la dpag pa) to proportion (the medicines) to the degree of the illness Lt.; sṅár-gyi rgyún-la (sngar gyi rgyun la) in comparison with the former time Tar.; rgyál-poi tugs-rje-la (rgyal po'i thugs rje la) by, or according to the king's favour; ṅai lúgs-la (nga'i lugs la) by my way of proceeding, according to my system Mil.; żábs-pyi-la (zhabs phyi la) (to go with a person) as a companion. — 4. most frq. la (la) is used as sign of the dat. case, col. also of the accus. following a vb. a. — 5. in all the relations mentioned above, la (la) is added to the inf., partic. and root of a vb., wherever the verb will at all admit of it, and besides it is used as gerundial particle in a similar sense as te (te): a. after the inf. (only in B.): lha-rtén źig yód-pa-la (lha rten zhig yod pa la) as there was in that place an idol-shrine Dzl.; often also to be translated by although. b. added to the root (B. and col.): mtóṅ-la ma btags (mthong la ma btags) (though) having seen it, yet he did not fasten it Dzl.; col. esp. when the root is doubled, for while, whilst: *ṅe śa tub-túb-la kyod śiṅ kur* (*nge sha tub tub la khyod shing khur*) fetch thou wood, whilst I am cutting the meat into pieces W.; in C. and B. = čiṅ (cing), also added to adjectives, lus mi-sdúg-čiṅ tuṅ-la dbyaṅs snyán-pa (lus mi sdug cing thung la dbyangs snyan pa) ugly as to his body (and) of small stature, (but) having a fine voice Dzl.; in sentences containing an imp. it is added to the root of it: śóg-la ltos śig (shog la ltos shig) come and look!

sealing-wax, Wdn., *la-kyir* (*la khyir*) W. balls of sealing-wax, with a hole for stringing them, used like our sticks of sealing-wax; la-tíg (la thig) drops of sealing-wax; la-tíg rgyág-pa (la thig rgyag pa) to drop melted sealing-wax upon (a person), as a torture.
Glr., either a sort of turnip, or (more prob.) for lá-p'ug daṅ nyúṅ-ma (la phug dang nyung ma) radish and turnip.
Hind. لتا ? an imported material like flax or a sort of linen-cloth, not in general use; hence in many parts of the country unknown.

wylie: la tu, la ṭu, la du,
english: prop. a sort of pastry of India, composed of suet, coarse meal, sugar and spices; the word may also be used for our gingerbread.

also már-yul (mar yul), Ladág, Ladák, province in the valley of the Indus between mṅa-rís (mnga' ris) and Bálti, inhabited by Tibetans and formerly belonging to Tibet, afterwards an independent kingdom, but recently conquered by Gulab Singh of Kashmere and hindooized as much as possible by his son and successor; capital Le.

wylie: la pa sha, la pa shag
english: Cs. a kind of upper garment without a girdle.
buttermilk, boiled, but not yet dried into vermicelli (čúr-ba) (chur ba).

radish, bod lá-p'ug (bod la phug) the common black radish, ni f.; rgya lá-p'ug (rgya la phug) a red species, of an acidulous taste. The carrot (Daucus carota) is in C. also col. called la-p'ug sér-po (la phug ser po). — la-bdár (la bdar), gen. *lab-
